title: GraphEvent
description: The real-time unit of change in Agent-Fabric. The graph is the left-fold of an ordered stream of GraphEvents. Each event upserts or removes a single entity or relation and carries the metadata needed for ordering, replay, and lineage.

stream.description: Logical stream/partition this event belongs to (e.g. per-tenant or per-workspace).

sequence.description: Monotonic per-stream sequence number. Defines total order within a stream.

op.description: Change operation. 'upsert' creates or updates; 'delete' tombstones; 'invalidate' closes valid-time without deletion.

target.description: Whether the event applies to a node (entity) or an edge (relation).

occurredAt.description: Valid-time: when the change happened in the world.

recordedAt.description: Transaction-time: when the fabric ingested the event.

source.description: Producer that emitted the event (connector, agent, platform service).

correlationId.description: Groups events belonging to the same logical operation/transaction.

causationId.description: Id of the event/command that directly caused this event, for lineage.

payload.description: The full entity or relation body. Required for 'upsert'. For 'delete'/'invalidate' an id-only stub is sufficient.
